suggestions.topic: REST-SSZ Engine API decision: PR #764 vs #793
suggestions.rationale: Deferred from ACDT #80 to ACDE Thursday, but likely needs ACDT follow-up. Nethermind and Erigon already merged #764; this creates implementation divergence risk if decision changes.

bal-devnet-7 targeted launch May 18-19; glamsterdam-devnet-4 requires separate branches with engine API gas limit changes (PR #796). Need confirmation of launch status, client readiness, and any blockers discovered.

ACDT #80 emphasized urgency: batch I/O and state root optimizations are blockers for final repricing numbers. Client teams were asked to report status in BAL Discord. These benchmarks gate EIP-7904 inclusion decision and final EIP-8038/2780 values.

EIP-8038, EIP-2780, EIP-8246, and EIP-7954 all target devnet-5 with placeholder numbers. Need to confirm scope, benchmark-dependent final values, and launch timeline. Code size limit (32/48/64KB) requires benchmarking before finalizing.

ACDT #80 noted most clients exceed 100M gas/s post-BAL optimizations, making EIP-7904 tentatively unnecessary. However, decision is gated on completing benchmarks. Need formal go/no-go based on latest data.

Open since ACDT #77 with action items in #78 and #79. Proposal allows CL sync without payload envelopes, creating security implications for EL validation. Writeup and formal EL team review still pending.

ACDT #79 reported Lighthouse sync issues, Teku envelope problems, and peer downscoring preventing recovery. May have required devnet-4. Need status update on whether issues were resolved or devnet was abandoned.

ACDT #79 discussed PR 5223 (8192 epoch delay) with cost analysis showing $250k attack cost considered low. Alternative EVM signature verification proposed. Justin Traglia tasked with presenting benchmarks at ACDC, but no resolution in ACDT series yet.
